entities.description: bookrag is a retrieval augmented generation method introduced to bridge a gap in document qa tasks
bookindex is a document native structure used by bookrag to organize information through hierarchical and graph based methods
document qa tasks are the specific problems that bookrag and bookindex are designed to address
the hierarchical tree structure is a method used to preserve the document s native logical hierarchy by organizing parsed content blocks
kg refers to a knowledge graph constructed to capture intricate relations within document blocks
the table of contents is the role served by the hierarchical tree structure in organizing the document s logical hierarchy
parsed content blocks are the units of document content organized into a hierarchical tree structure
fine grained entities are the specific data points contained within the document blocks that are captured by the knowledge graph
the relation refers to the deep connections within the document that the method aims to capture
tree nodes are the specific components of the hierarchical tree structure to which kg entities are mapped

relations.description: bookrag is built upon the document native bookindex
bookrag is designed specifically for document qa tasks
bookindex organizes information using a hierarchical tree structure to preserve logical hierarchy
bookindex constructs a kg to capture intricate relations within document blocks
the hierarchical tree structure serves the role of the document s table of contents
the kg entities are mapped to their corresponding tree nodes to unify the two structures
bookindex organizes parsed content blocks into a hierarchical tree structure
the kg is constructed containing fine grained entities to capture intricate relations
bookrag is designed to capture the deep connection of the relation in the document
kg entities are mapped to their corresponding tree nodes to unify the structures
parsed content blocks are organized into the hierarchical tree structure which consists of tree nodes
